name: Pets De Soeurs (Sister's, I.e., Nun's, Farts)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the butter, sugar, salt, and lemon rind with 1 cup water in a saucepan. Bring the mixture to a boil slowly.
2. Remove the pan from the heat once the butter has melted completely.
3. In separate custard cups or small dishes, prepare the eggs by breaking them individually.
4. When the pan is removed from the heat, add all the flour at once and stir first carefully, then vigorously with a wooden spoon until absorbed.
5. Create a compact, thick paste. Return the pan to medium-high heat and cook for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly and scraping the sides and bottom of the pan until the batter clings together in a solid mass without leaving the pan dirty and glossy.
6. Turn off the heat and remove the pan from the stove.
7. Beat in vanilla and rum (if used), allowing the batter to cool slightly.
8. Make a well in the center of the batter, add 1 egg at a time, and beat each into the mass until all eggs are incorporated. The resulting pastry should be flexible, soft, firm enough to hold its shape, and not runny. Allow it to rest for about 45 minutes or until supper is ready.
9. To make the fritters, heat oil in a deep skillet or deep-fat fryer to 360°F (not too hot; otherwise, the exterior may brown before the interior cooks).
10. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil using a slotted spoon or wire mesh skimmer. Do not overcrowd the pan as they expand to about four times their original size.
11. Gently nudge them to ensure even coloring on all sides. When golden brown, drain on paper towels and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar. Serve hot.
12. Pet de Breton: Preheat an oven to 375°F. Pour the batter into a greased pan (a 10-inch heavy cast-iron frying pan works well) and bake for an hour. Remove, sprinkle with confectioners' sugar, tear apart, and serve when cool enough to handle.
